compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Code review reviews each Gerrit patchset (and each local `rebar review-code` run) fresh, with no memory across runs. Plan review already has a novelty rising-floor convergence mechanism (ADR 0008): on a re-review it loads the prior run's SURFACED findings, an LLM novelty sub-call scores each current finding against those priors, and a deterministic predicate drops NOVEL + low-priority findings so the remediation loop converges instead of surfacing fresh trivia each pass. This epic brings a comparable, machinery-reusing novelty mechanism to code review, adapted for its one key difference: code findings carry source citations, so the floor is gated per-finding on whether the cited code REGION changed since the last review.

A durable, git-shared `code_review` artifact (per Gerrit change/revision) already persists findings as a write-only calibration corpus (limestone-unethical-zebrafinch). This epic adds the READER and the region gate that consume it, plus local-review persistence.

## Design (settled)
- REUSE boundary is the contract, not the prompt. Code review calls TWO pure kernel primitives UNCHANGED: the novelty SCORING contract (novelty_model / NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS in review_kernel/verify.py) and rising_floor_drop(priority, novelty) (review_kernel/decide.py). The novelty PROMPT is gate-specific and is NEW work: the existing plan_review_novelty.md uses the {{plan}} template var and is plan-scoped, so code review adds its own novelty reviewer prompt (code_review_novelty.md, oriented to a diff + a finding list) and a new reviewer id (code-novelty), exactly as the base/overlay reviewer prompts already differ per gate. Runner wiring mirrors plan-review's novelty sub-call: the novelty system prompt carries the diff as domain context, the surfaced prior findings are appended to the instructions as a context block, and the call binds output_schema='code_review_novelty' (the reused kernel contract). The cites_prior_location sub-question is adapted to source-file/line region semantics. Exact prompt front-matter, template-variable names, and reviewer-index registration are implementation details resolved by the child against the live reviewer index / runner conventions — the plan fixes the mechanism (diff-as-context + prior-findings-in-instructions + reused novelty contract), not an unwritten prompt's field names. A new region_gated_floor (code_review-specific) wraps rising_floor_drop with a third conjunct. The reused novelty contract is registered for code review at code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts() (importing novelty_model from review_kernel.verify), mirroring plan_review/passes.py::register_contracts(); without it a RunRequest(output_schema='code_review_novelty') fails at runtime. No shared novelty-engine abstraction spanning reader/eligibility/gating, since those genuinely differ per gate.
- Novelty is scored ONLY against findings returned to the client (surfaced block/advisory), never against dropped/hidden findings (surfaced-only; the plan-review side is old-frilly-plankton).
- Permanent drop of low-impact findings is the intended convergence compromise; the priority floor is the escape hatch that keeps surfacing higher-impact findings. Trust the priority score: no categorical exemption, and recalibration lives on the priority axis, not the novelty axis. No eval-gated activation flag.
- Region gate is content-addressed and resilient (survives rebase/force-push, needs no reachable commit): store a per-reviewed-file content-hash map {path: sha256} in the artifact. Reuse boundary: the private _hash_file primitive from plan-review attest.py is reused; dependency_hashes() itself is NOT (it is plan/ticket-coupled — pulls ticket file_impact, resolves plan-verdict citation buckets, and a pinned attested snapshot), so code review writes a thin dep collector over its changed_files that calls _hash_file per path; at the next run, re-hash the current files and compare. The region state is a DEFINED vocabulary, not prose: constants REGION_UNCHANGED / REGION_CHANGED / REGION_UNKNOWN (in code_review/region_gate.py), referenced by the predicate and the hash-compare. Only REGION_UNCHANGED enables a drop; REGION_UNKNOWN behaves as raise. Absence-evidence, multi-location, moved, and renamed-file findings all resolve to REGION_UNKNOWN. File-level for v1; line-level (reviewdog added semantics via git diff --no-index against a stored snapshot) is a later refinement.
- Fail-safe: region_gated_floor is wrapped in try/except. Any failure in the reader (artifact load), the current-file hashing, or the comparison resolves affected regions to REGION_UNKNOWN and returns the FULL unfiltered finding set (no drops) — mirroring plan-review's _maybe_apply_rising_floor / _score_floor_novelty fail-safes. A broken signal can only make the gate stricter, never suppress more. A prior artifact lacking the deps map (an older v1 payload) yields REGION_UNKNOWN for all findings -> no drops.
- Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from (the prior run/patchset key) and are OMITTED from coaching (not re-coached); they remain surfaced. Dropped novel-on-unchanged findings move to the verdict dropped bucket. drop_reason reuses the existing verdict field (plan-review uses the value "novelty"); code review ADDS the value "novelty-region" to that same vocabulary.
- Keying + addressing: local reviews are keyed on the session id (resolve_session_id(), with a per-invocation uuid fallback when it returns None); Gerrit reviews are keyed on the change_id, which SPANS revisions/patchsets: each patchset emits its own artifact titled "code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}", and the reader for patchset N+1 queries prior revisions of the SAME change_id (the change is the memory key; the revision only makes each artifact idempotent). Artifacts are addressed by title: Gerrit uses "code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}" (existing); local uses "code-review: session:{session_id}", and session_id is ALSO a top-level payload field. TYPED keyspaces (session:<id> vs change:<id>); each reader matches only its own title prefix / key, so a prior LOCAL review never seeds a change's FIRST Gerrit review.
- uuid-fallback rationale (conscious tradeoff, not a gap): a bare `rebar review-code` with no session lifecycle (resolve_session_id() -> None) mints a fresh uuid per invocation, so local convergence is intentionally INERT there — isolation is chosen over convergence to guarantee no cross-session contamination. Genuine per-session local convergence is delivered wherever a session lifecycle exists: any coding-agent harness (native session var, validated shared-across-subagents + unique-per-session) or a SessionStart shim exporting REBAR_SESSION_ID (docs/session-id-shims.md) for harnesses lacking a native var.
- Neutrality preserved: the Pass-1 finder never sees prior findings; only the post-find novelty seam does (ADR 0008 Invariant 1).

## Success Criteria
- On a re-run of a review with NO code changes to the cited files, every prior low-priority SURFACED finding is stamped carried_from and NOT re-coached, and is not re-surfaced as novel — verifiable by the region_gated_floor test harness. (This is the observable proxy for "repeat-noise reduced".)
- Novel low-priority findings on UNCHANGED code are dropped for convergence; novel findings on CHANGED code, and all high-priority findings, are always raised at full strength.
- Local and Gerrit finding memory are isolated namespaces: a prior local review does not alter a change's first Gerrit review verdict.
- Achieved WITHOUT importing dso's heavy cycle machinery (arbiter, relation taxonomy, oscillation loop).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A reader loads prior SURFACED (decision in {block, advisory}) code-review findings by typed key (session/change).
- [ ] The reader returns the reviewed-file content-hash map from the resolved artifact.
- [ ] The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id as a top-level field.
- [ ] The code_review_result_v1 payload carries a {path: sha256} deps map.
- [ ] Local artifacts use the "code-review: session:{session_id}" title scheme.
- [ ] Local `rebar review-code` persists a code_review artifact (today only the Gerrit voter does).
- [ ] region_gated_floor reuses the novelty sub-call and rising_floor_drop unchanged (no fork).
- [ ] region_gated_floor drops a finding only when it is novel AND low-priority AND REGION_UNCHANGED.
- [ ] region_gated_floor never drops on REGION_CHANGED.
- [ ] region_gated_floor never drops on REGION_UNKNOWN.
- [ ] Carryover findings are stamped with the carried_from field.
- [ ] Carryover findings are omitted from coaching while remaining surfaced.
- [ ] A reader/hash/compare error returns the full unfiltered finding set (no drops).
- [ ] Local review memory is keyed on the session id (with a per-invocation uuid fallback).
- [ ] Gerrit review memory is keyed on the Gerrit change.
- [ ] A test verifies a prior local review does not change a change's first Gerrit review verdict.
- [ ] c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only.
- [ ] Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only.
- [ ] The ADR records the explicit decision that NO arbiter / relation-taxonomy / oscillation-loop machinery is added and why the lightweight novelty floor suffices (positively discharging the no-dso-cycle-machinery success criterion; this is the epic's original oscillation/convergence-signal decision, made by design rather than deferred).
compiled_state.title: Cross-patchset code-review finding memory (deferred from sure-foyer-aroma)
